spans.content: Data-efficient Large Vision Models through Sequential Autoregression
Zhiwei Hao*1, Jianyuan Guo*2, Chengcheng Wang*3
1School of information and Electronics, Beijing Institute of Technology.
2School of Computer Science, Faculty of Engineering, The University of Sydney.
Training general-purpose large multimodal foundation modelson purely sequential data,similar to linguistic
inputs,has heraldedanew frontier in visual perception. However,current endeavors are hamstrung byan
over-reliance on colossal models，exemplifiedby the necessity foran extensive corpus of data，often
based foundationmodel,innovatively architected tooperate ona limited dataset.Ourevaluations demonstrate
how this model achieves proficiency in a spectrum of visual tasks spanning both high-level and low-level
semantic understanding.Witha significant reduction in parameter footprint,andamarked decrease in training
datarequirements，we pave the way for more sustainable and accessible advancements in the field of
Part-1: Data-efficient Training: Data Augmentation Strategy
Part-2: Low Power Consumption & Tiny Model: Knowledge Distillation
The introduction of KD proves beneficial in enhancing the single-task performance of LVMs.
The introduction of KD proves beneficial in enhancing the multi-task performance of LVMs.
